seo.title: Fund the AI adoption you can't afford to skip · MDF, AWS, Google, EU grants
seo.description: Most companies qualify for Microsoft MDF, AWS co-sell, Google Cloud Partner funds or EU digital grants. Few structure the application right. We do that, free, on a call.
seo.keywords: Microsoft MDF, AWS co-sell, Google Cloud Partner Advantage, Gemini Enterprise funding, EU digital grants, AI funding Romania, Digital Europe Programme, vendor credits, AI training grant, mid-market funding EU

hero.title: You learned what to build. Now fund building it.
hero.subtitle: Training leaves your team with working prototypes and the capability to use them. Production — agents, integrations, licences, dev work, change management — costs real money. Microsoft, AWS, Google and EU programmes fund exactly this transition. Most companies qualify. Few structure their programme in a way the funding body recognises.
hero.ctaText: Find out what you qualify for →
hero.note: Free · No commitment · Written shortlist in 5 business days

steps.label: Training
steps.title: Your team builds working prototypes on real workflows.
steps.body: In our programmes, teams leave with prototypes built on their own problems. Not slides about AI. Actual working builds. The knowledge and the artefacts stay in-house.

steps.label: The gap
steps.title: A prototype is not a production system.
steps.body: Turning a prototype into a deployed agent or workflow requires licences, integrations, dev work, and change management. Most adoption stalls here. Not on knowledge. On budget.

steps.label: Funding
steps.title: The budget for this already exists.
steps.body: Microsoft, AWS, Google and EU digital grants were designed specifically to fund this transition. We structure your programme so it qualifies, and we draft the application. The work happens either way. The funding determines whether it costs you anything.

legal.label: EU AI Act · Article 4 · Compliance requirement
legal.body: Article 4 of the EU AI Act requires organisations using AI systems to ensure adequate AI literacy across their workforce. This is not a recommendation. It is a legal obligation. Compliance training programmes qualify under multiple vendor and EU funding instruments. The cost of non-compliance exceeds the cost of a funded training programme. The funding conversation gets straightforward: you are legally required to do this anyway.

paragraphs: Every quarter, Microsoft, AWS, and Google allocate Market Development Funds to partners who drive platform adoption. These budgets are designed to support AI skills and implementation programmes inside organisations. Most of them go unused. Not because companies don't qualify, but because nobody structured the programme in a way that meets the funding criteria.

The same applies to EU digital competitiveness grants under Digital Europe and related programmes. Applications fail not because the work isn't fundable, but because they are submitted as internal training plans instead of qualifying projects. The framing is wrong. The work is right.
We turn your AI adoption programme into something the funding body recognises. You run the programme. You get the cost back.
objection.highlight: The budget exists. It just hasn't been identified or structured correctly. Every quarter, this money sits unused while companies tell us they can't afford implementation.

instruments.titleEm: Most companies have access to at least one.
instruments.intro: These are current, named, active programmes — not generic categories. Credit amounts, partner tiers, and eligibility windows shift quarterly. We track them so you don't have to.

items.title: Microsoft MDF & Founders Hub
items.body: Partners: Market Development Funds support Copilot, Azure AI, and Microsoft 365 adoption. Programmes must be structured around specific adoption outcomes. Startups: Founders Hub provides up to $150k in Azure and OpenAI credits. We facilitate access to both tracks.
coverage: Azure AI and OpenAI API consumption
Up to $150k credits via Founders Hub

items.title: AWS co-sell & Activate credits
items.body: APN partners: co-sell budgets fund digital skills and cloud adoption programmes aligned to AWS metrics. Startups: AWS Activate provides up to $100k in credits including Bedrock and SageMaker access. We facilitate access to both tracks.

items.title: Google Cloud partner & startup credits
items.body: Partners: Google Cloud Partner Advantage allocates co-marketing and adoption funds for Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, and Workspace rollouts. Startups: Google for Startups Cloud Program offers up to $200k in credits and dedicated AI support.
coverage: Up to $200k cloud credits over 2 years
MVP build and partner delivery credits

items.title: EU digital grants & Erasmus+
items.body: EU funding for AI literacy, digital skills and implementation work inside organisations. Includes PNRR Romania digitalization components, POCIDIF, Digital Europe and Erasmus+ upskilling cohorts. Applications must be framed as qualifying projects with measurable outcomes.

programmes.intro: When we say “four sources” we mean these specific programmes — current, named, active in 2026. Vendor credits on the left, EU and national grants on the right. Not exhaustive — open calls and partner programmes shift every quarter.

items.name: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub
items.what: Up to $150k Azure + OpenAI credits. No equity required.

items.name: Microsoft MDF (Market Development Funds)
items.what: Partner-led co-funding for Copilot, Azure AI and Microsoft 365 adoption programmes.

items.name: AWS Activate
items.what: Up to $100k AWS credits including Bedrock model access.

items.name: AWS co-sell & partner funding
items.what: APN partner budgets for digital skills and cloud adoption programmes.

items.name: Google for Startups Cloud Program
items.what: Up to $200k Google Cloud + Gemini credits over 2 years.

items.name: Google Cloud Partner Advantage
items.what: Co-marketing and adoption funds for Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, Workspace.

items.name: Anthropic for Startups
items.what: Claude API credits and priority access for early-stage teams.

items.name: PNRR Romania — digitalizare
items.what: National Recovery and Resilience Plan, digitalization components. Romania-specific, active calls in 2025–2026.

items.name: POCIDIF
items.what: Operational Programme Smart Growth, Digitalization and Financial Instruments. Covers AI deployment and upskilling projects.

items.name: Digital Europe Programme
items.what: EU co-funding for AI skills, digital testing environments and EDIH access.

items.name: Horizon Europe — AI calls
items.what: R&D and innovation funding including AI-focused topics and consortium projects.

items.name: Erasmus+
items.what: Upskilling cohorts, training mobility and cross-border partnerships for workforce development.

programmes.footnote: Not exhaustive. We track more — open calls and partner programmes shift every quarter.

steps.title: Audit your vendor relationships
steps.body: We review your Microsoft Partner status, AWS tier, Google Cloud Partner Advantage standing, and any existing vendor agreements to identify which programmes you qualify for right now.

steps.title: Map eligible instruments
steps.body: Based on the audit, we identify the specific MDF programmes, co-sell budgets, or EU grant instruments that fit your situation. Not a generic list — a specific set with qualification criteria confirmed.

steps.title: Structure the programme to qualify
steps.body: We redesign the scope and documentation of your AI adoption programme to meet the funding criteria. The programme content stays the same. The way it is framed and documented changes. This is the step most companies miss.

steps.title: Draft and submit the application
steps.body: We write or co-author the MDF proposal or grant application. Vendor fund applications require specific language and outcome metrics. We know what those are because we have done this for our own programmes.

steps.title: Align delivery and reimbursement
steps.body: Delivery documentation is built to match application requirements from day one. Reimbursement does not stall because the paperwork does not match what was submitted.

result.label: Result
result.body: A fully funded, or significantly reduced-cost, adoption programme. For companies where internal budget approval is the blocker, this removes the objection. For companies where budget exists but is hard to defend, qualifying for vendor or EU funding shifts the conversation from “should we spend this?” to “should we leave this on the table?”

paragraphs: We are an AI adoption company. The funding audit and application structuring is not a standalone paid service. It is part of how we engage with companies who want to run our programmes but need the budget approved first.

We structure the funding because we deliver the programme. Vendor funds cover licences, integrations and implementation. TUS is the implementation partner. The funding application names us as the delivery vendor. That is the arrangement, stated plainly.
If you qualify for nothing, we tell you that in the written shortlist within 5 business days of the scoping call. No pitch follows. No commitment was made.

points.label: What the call covers
points.body: We map your vendor relationships and EU eligibility. You leave with a clear picture of which instruments apply, estimated coverage amounts, and what documentation you need. No commitment until you see the numbers.

points.label: What happens after
points.body: If you qualify and want to proceed, we structure the programme, draft the application, and coordinate delivery. TUS is the named delivery partner in the application.

points.label: What it costs you
points.body: The structuring and application work is covered under the implementation agreement. If the funding is not approved, you do not pay for the structuring work.

points.label: Our incentive
points.body: We only get paid to deliver a programme when you have the budget to fund one. Your funding approval is our commercial interest. The incentives are aligned.

engine.intro: The structuring work isn't a single PDF. It runs on an operational layer we use across every funding engagement. We'll open it up as a separate tool later — for now it runs inside our work for you.

items.title: Continuous scanning
items.body: We monitor EU and national calls weekly and flag the ones that match your profile.

items.title: Fit scoring
items.body: Each opportunity is scored against your org profile — eligibility, effort, expected coverage.

items.title: Deadline & follow-up tracking
items.body: Every call has a clock. We track submission windows, milestones and follow-up requirements so nothing slips.

items.title: Reuse of past content
items.body: Drafts pull from your previous applications, decks and proposals — so you refine instead of starting from a blank page.

items.title: Structured proposal drafting
items.body: Each section of the application is drafted in the format the funding body expects, ready for your team to refine.

items.title: Partner & co-applicant coordination
items.body: Where the call needs a consortium, we help line up the co-applicants and coordinate the joint submission.

items.q: How long does an MDF application take?
items.a:
From kick-off to submission: typically 3–5 weeks. One week for audit and instrument mapping, 1–2 weeks for programme structuring, 1–2 weeks for drafting and partner sign-off. The vendor's own decision window adds another 2–6 weeks on top of submission.

EU grant timelines are longer and vary by instrument. PNRR and POCIDIF calls have fixed submission windows. We track these deadlines and flag them before they close.

items.q: What if we are not a Microsoft, AWS or Google partner yet?
items.a:
You don't need to be a formal partner to access the startup and scaleup tracks. AWS Activate, Google for Startups, and Microsoft Founders Hub are all accessible without partner certification. For enterprise MDF, being an existing customer of their cloud or workspace products is typically sufficient.

We check exactly which tier you are in during the scoping call and map the fastest path to the instruments that apply to your situation.

For vendor credits (AWS, Google, Microsoft Founders Hub), the funds are applied directly to your billing account to offset infrastructure and API costs. Nothing to claim — the credit is there when you need it.

For EU grants and partner MDF: you run the programme, pay the invoices, and submit proof of execution. The vendor or EU body reimburses you directly. We structure the delivery documentation from day one to match the reimbursement criteria exactly — not retrofitted at the end.

items.q: What documents do you need from us?
items.a:
For the initial audit: nothing except 30 minutes of your time. We need to understand your current technology stack and vendor relationships. That's the whole input for the scoping call.

Once we identify a fundable track, we will need your standard company profile and, where applicable, read-only access to your vendor partner portal. We provide a specific document checklist at that stage.

The funding audit and application structuring are free. Why? Because we are an AI adoption company, not a grants consultancy. We do the heavy lifting to get your budget approved with one condition: once the funding is secured, The Unlearning School is your partner for delivering the AI adoption programme.

We bring the structure that unlocks the budget. You run the programme with us. That is the arrangement, stated plainly.

items.q: What if we don't qualify for anything?
items.a:
We tell you immediately on the scoping call. We don't spend time structuring applications that won't win, because we only get paid when you actually run the programme with us. If the funding is not there, we say so and part ways.

In practice, most mid-market companies in Romania qualify for at least one instrument. PNRR digitalization tracks and Erasmus+ upskilling cohorts cover a wide range of situations. We find out in 30 minutes.

eventsBridge.titleStart: Want to see what a session produces
eventsBridge.body: Join a live prototyping session and see the exact deliverables — working prototypes, team capability built, practical artefacts — before you structure funding around it.

quote.body: Most companies qualify for at least one instrument. The question is not whether the funding exists — it does. The question is whether your programme is structured in a way that the funding body will recognise.

cta.title: Find out what you qualify for.
cta.subtitle: A 30-minute call. A written shortlist in 5 days. We map your vendor relationships and EU eligibility in a single call. You get a written shortlist of instruments, estimated coverage, and next steps within 5 business days. No commitment until you see the numbers.
